principle: Use a mega-menu for sites with more than two levels of navigation hierarchy — nested dropdown menus require precise mouse control and fail on touch devices
explanation: X.ux|mega_menu: nested_dropdowns: require_mouse_path_precision -> hover_lost_on_pixel_deviation; mega_menu: flat_grid_of_all_options_in_one_panel -> no_hover_path_required; trigger: click_or_300ms_hover_intent_NOT_immediate_hover; structure: column_headers+items_below; touch: mega_menu_opens_on_tap; max_items_per_column: 7; NO:nested_3-level_dropdown NO:hover_only_trigger_without_delay; eg: 3-level_nested_dropdown >> flat_mega_menu_panel_with_column_headers

principle: Make navigation sticky only for task-oriented sites — sticky navigation helps users who navigate mid-task, but adds visual weight to editorial content like articles and blogs
explanation: X.ux|sticky_nav: sticky_always: navigation_competes_with_content_on_long_editorial_pages; task_sites(SaaS+ecom+dashboard): user_needs_nav_mid_task -> sticky_helps; editorial_sites(blog+news+docs): user_reads_linearly -> hide_on_scroll_down+show_on_scroll_up(shrink_variant); implementation: CSS:position:sticky+top:0 OR IntersectionObserver_for_scroll_direction; NO:full_height_sticky_nav_on_article_pages NO:no_sticky_on_long_form_checkout; eg: full_sticky_nav_on_blog >> hide_on_scroll_down+reveal_on_scroll_up

principle: Add breadcrumbs on any page more than two levels deep — users who land on deep pages via search have no other way to understand where they are in the site hierarchy
explanation: X.ux|breadcrumbs: deep_landing_without_breadcrumbs: SEO_visitor_disoriented -> bounces; breadcrumb_rules: show_full_path_from_homepage+current_page_NOT_linked; mobile: truncate_middle_with_ellipsis+show_root_and_current; schema: BreadcrumbList_JSON-LD -> rich_results; placement: directly_above_page_heading; NOT_needed: flat_sites(1-2_levels) NOT_needed: homepage; NO:breadcrumbs_only_for_decoration NO:current_page_as_link; eg: no_breadcrumbs_on_category_page >> Home > Category > Subcategory > [Current Page]

principle: Use a left sidebar for documentation and dashboard navigation — sidebar navigation exposes all sections simultaneously, eliminating the need to navigate to discover available options
explanation: X.ux|sidebar_nav: top_nav_for_docs: hides_structure_in_hamburger -> user_cant_see_all_sections; sidebar: persistent_left_panel(240-280px)+all_sections_visible+active_state+collapsible_groups; mobile: sidebar_converts_to_drawer(hamburger_trigger); active_section: highlighted+expanded; scroll: sidebar_scrolls_independently; NO:hamburger_menu_for_documentation NO:top_nav_for_10+_sections; eg: docs_with_top_nav+hidden_sections >> left_sidebar_with_all_chapters_visible

principle: Include a visible skip-to-main-content link as the first focusable element — keyboard and screen reader users navigate through the header on every page without it
explanation:
X.ux|skip_link: keyboard_user: must_tab_through_entire_nav_every_page_without_skip_link; WCAG_2.4.1_bypass_blocks: required_for_compliance; implementation: Skip to content; visually_hidden_until_focused: .skip-link{position:absolute;top:-100%} .skip-link:focus{top:0}; anchor:
; NO:skip_link_missing NO:skip_link_present_but_non-functional; eg: nav_without_skip_link >> skip_link_as_first_tab_stop_appears_on_focus

principle: Add hover intent delay of 200–300ms before opening navigation dropdowns — immediate hover triggers cause dropdowns to open while users are moving the mouse across the page
explanation: X.ux|hover_intent: immediate_hover_trigger: dropdown_opens_while_mouse_traverses_nav -> unwanted_panel_flash; hover_intent: fire_only_if_mouse_dwells_200-300ms_on_trigger; CSS:pure_hover: no_delay_possible -> JS_required; implementation: hoverIntent_library OR CSS_transition-delay_on_visibility; touch: open_on_tap_first_touch NOT on_hover; NO:dropdown_open_on_mouseenter_immediately NO:500ms+_delay_that_feels_unresponsive; eg: dropdown_opens_on_mouse_enter >> opens_after_200ms_hover_dwell

principle: Make the current section visually distinct in navigation — users who cannot see where they are in the site lose orientation, especially those who land from search
explanation: X.ux|active_nav_state: no_active_state: user_disoriented_on_landing -> increased_bounce; active_state: current_page_or_section: font-weight_bold+underline+color_change+background_indicator; breadcrumb_reinforces_position; sidebar_nav: highlight_current_item+expand_current_section; multi-level: highlight_both_parent_and_child; aria: aria-current="page" on active link; NO:no_visual_distinction_for_active_nav_item NO:active_color_too_subtle_to_notice; eg: all_nav_items_same_color >> current_item_bold+underline+primary_color

principle: Prioritize a search box over navigation when the site has more than 200 pages — large catalogs are impossible to navigate fully via hierarchical menus
explanation: X.ux|search_vs_nav: >200_pages: navigation_hierarchy_cannot_surface_all_content; search: most_efficient_path_for_known_item_lookup; persistent_search: place_in_header_visible_on_all_pages; autocomplete: show_top_5_suggestions_with_categories; no-results: suggest_related_content+popular_searches; categories_still_needed: for_browse_discovery; NO:relying_solely_on_nav_for_large_catalogs NO:search_hidden_in_hamburger; eg: navigation_only_for_500_product_catalog >> persistent_header_search+category_navigation
